assertion_results.text: Output must mention CI run time of 47 minutes
assertion_results.evidence: Priority 2.1: 'Break the 47-minute build into parallelized stages.' CI/CD Pipeline scored 1.5/5: 'A 47-minute build from a 2011 Jenkins setup is a serious bottleneck.'

assertion_results.text: Output must address flaky tests specifically (not just 'tests exist')
assertion_results.evidence: Priority 1.1: 'Flaky tests are not a test problem — they are a trust problem. When engineers learn to ignore red builds, the entire CI signal is compromised.' Test Suite Health: '30% flakiness renders roughly 3,600 tests unreliable. Ignored tests are worse than no tests — they create false confidence.'

assertion_results.text: Output must give at least one short-term action item
assertion_results.evidence: Priority 1 section ('Stop the Bleeding, Weeks 1–6') contains three concrete action items including quarantine flaky tests and enforce hotfix review policy.

assertion_results.text: Output must score CI/CD at 2/5 or lower
assertion_results.evidence: CI/CD Pipeline scored 1.5/5: 'A 47-minute build from a 2011 Jenkins setup is a serious bottleneck.' (1.5 ≤ 2)

assertion_results.text: Output must not recommend replacing Jenkins without acknowledging migration cost
assertion_results.evidence: Priority 2.1: 'consider migrating from Jenkins to a modern pipeline tool (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, or Buildkite). Do not undertake a full migration immediately — start by parallelizing within Jenkins to prove the time reduction before platform investment.'
